summaries.No Black Box Anymore: Demystifying Clini: This paper likely proposes an interpretable clinical prediction framework using a temporal-feature cross-attention mechanism to reveal how patient variables and their timing jointly drive model predictions.
summaries.SkillsBench: Benchmarking how well agent: This paper likely introduces SkillsBench, a benchmark for systematically evaluating how effectively reusable agent skills generalize and perform across a wide range of tasks and environments.
summaries.Consistency of Large Reasoning Models Un: This paper likely studies whether large reasoning models maintain stable answers and behavior when subjected to adversarial manipulations over multiple conversational turns.
summaries.Time-To-Inconsistency: A Survival Analys: This paper likely applies survival analysis to measure how long large language models remain robust before becoming inconsistent or vulnerable under repeated adversarial attacks.
summaries.Chain-of-Influence: Tracing Interdepende: This paper likely presents a method for tracing how clinical features influence one another over time in predictive models, improving interpretability of longitudinal healthcare predictions.
summaries.Co-Alignment: Rethinking Alignment as Bi: This paper likely argues that AI alignment should be viewed as a two-way process in which both humans and AI systems adapt to each other's cognitive patterns and goals.
summaries.Enhancing End-Stage Renal Disease Outcom: This paper likely develops a predictive model for end-stage renal disease outcomes by integrating multiple data sources to improve accuracy and clinical usefulness.
summaries.Beyond single-turn: A survey on multi-tu: This paper likely surveys research on multi-turn interactions with large language models, covering dialogue dynamics, memory, planning, evaluation, and emerging challenges beyond single-turn prompting.
summaries.Firm or Fickle? Evaluating Large Languag: This paper likely evaluates whether large language models remain consistent in their responses, preferences, or reasoning across sequential conversational interactions.
summaries.Towards Interpretable End-Stage Renal Di: This paper likely explores explainable AI methods for predicting end-stage renal disease from administrative claims data, with an emphasis on making risk predictions more interpretable for healthcare use.
summaries.Confidence as Control: A Survey of Confi: This paper likely surveys how confidence estimates are represented and used in large language models for tasks such as calibration, decision-making, control, and uncertainty-aware interaction.
